title: Computer Networking: A Top-Down Approach
subtitle: A Top-Down Approach
author: James F. Kurose
themes: Layered network architecture from application down to physical
Internet protocols and standards as the unifying framework
Socket programming and network application development
Principles of reliable data transfer and congestion control
awards: Widely adopted as the standard networking textbook worldwide
IEEE James H. Mulligan, Jr. Education Medal (Kurose, 2020)
excerpt: Unique among networking texts, this book uses a top-down, layered approach — starting at the application layer and working down to the physical layer — to teach the fundamental principles of computer networking through the lens of the Internet.
